item.title: Former Afghan general charged with drug and firearm offenses in U.S.
item.description: A former member of Afghanistan's National Assembly was arrested and charged with conspiring to illegally import heroin and methamphetamine into the U.S.

item.title: Trump again says Iran ceasefire is over, but U.S. will keep talking
item.description: Efforts are underway to get U.S.-Iran peace talks back on track after the most intense exchange of attacks since the ceasefire took effect.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for July 12, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include White House border czar Tom Homan and retired Gen. Frank McKenzie, president of The Citadel and former commander of U.S. Central Command.

item.title: Michigan's cyclosporiasis outbreak grows to more than 1,500 cases
item.description: Michigan health officials say the state's cyclosporiasis outbreak has grown to more than 1,500 cases.

item.title: Charlie Kirk's family cites "overwhelming evidence" against alleged killer
item.description: Charlie Kirk's family said the end of Tyler Robinson's preliminary hearing "marks an important step forward in the pursuit of justice."

item.title: Man partially sucked out of plane window during flight, passenger says
item.description: A woman says she saw a fellow passenger on her Ryanair flight get his head and shoulders sucked out of a window that broke during their trip between Greece and Germany.

item.title: What we know about Iran's apparent plot to assassinate President Trump
item.description: Israeli officials tipped off the Trump administration about an apparent Iranian plot to assassinate him as the war between the two countries continues, according to a report from The Wall Street Journal. CBS News Charlie D'Agata breaks down what we know.

item.title: White House considering plan to add permanent fencing on Pennsylvania Ave.
item.description: In the plan under discussion, the Secret Service and the White House would be able to open and close sections of the fencing, sources said.

item.title: Prince Harry, Meghan visit King Charles after security controversy
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan have a rocky relationship with the prince's father.

item.title: Eight indicted over plans to attack Trump UFC event at White House
item.description: Eight people have each been charged with conspiracy counts over a planned attack at the UFC event that was held at the White House in June. CBS News' Jake Rosen reports.

item.title: Vance family to rent home in Virginia countryside
item.description: Sources familiar with the decision say the rental property would supplement — not replace — the official vice presidential residence at the Naval Observatory in Washington.

item.title: Witnesses who saw ICE agents kill Lorenzo Salgado Araujo disagree with DHS statement, attorney says
item.description: New details are emerging about what led to Lorenzo Salgado Araujo's death. The Mexican man was wrongfully shot and killed by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents during a traffic stop in Houston, Texas. Shawna Mizelle has the latest.

item.title: Trump admin. to back tariffs on Russian oil in effort to end Ukraine war, sources say
item.description: The war in Ukraine is now in its fifth year. A bipartisan bill would impose heavy financial penalties on purchasers of Russian oil,

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Passenger almost sucked out of plane after window breaks during flight
item.description: A passenger on a Ryanair flight was nearly sucked out of the plane's window midair. Kamal Afzali reports for CBS News from London.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $1,000 money market account will earn savers now
item.description: Have $1,000 that you want to deposit into a money market account? Here's how much interest you can earn if you do.

item.title: Man, 26, arrested after former British politician found dead in her home
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former member of Parliament, had "sustained serious injuries" when she was found dead in her home in southwest England, police said.

item.title: Gas prices start climbing again as U.S.-Iran tensions flare
item.description: Prices at the pump sank following a June ceasefire. But renewed conflict in the Middle East and other factors are driving fuel costs back up.

item.title: Scaled-back Alibaba settlement reflects DOJ's approach to food and drug cases
item.description: The scaled-backed Alibaba settlement reflects a broader trend by the DOJ of pulling back on criminal enforcement of corporate cases involving the safety of food, drugs, and medical devices.

item.title: Trump not expected to veto housing bill, U.S. official says
item.description: President Trump said Friday he won't sign the law, but a U.S. official said he isn't expected to veto it either.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Trump refuses to sign bipartisan housing bill in "protest" over SAVE America Act
item.description: President Trump announced on social media Friday that he is withholding his signature on a bipartisan housing affordability bill "in protest" because his SAVE America Act has yet to pass the Senate. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports from Washington, D.C.

item.title: Why AI content is flooding social media and taking over LinkedIn
item.description: A new report from AI detector Pangram found that AI-generated content is flooding socials like X and Reddit, with LinkedIn accounting for nearly two-thirds of all AI content detected. Pangram CEO and co-founder Max Spero joins CBS News to discuss his findings.

item.title: Parents of teen found dead on island don't believe he chose to stay behind
item.description: Christine and Elmore Wonsley said they don't believe their son would've stayed on the island when his friends left by boat.

item.title: What's the cheapest way to pay for a new central AC this summer?
item.description: The right financing option can help you avoid paying far more than your new air conditioner actually costs.

item.title: Alibaba sold dangerous drugs to Americans but avoided DOJ prosecution, public records show
item.description: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba knowingly sold dangerous drugs to U.S. consumers, but the DOJ did not prosecute, according to public records probed for a CBS News investigation. Senior Justice Department reporter Sarah Lynch breaks down her reporting and why charges weren't pursued.

item.title: Appeals court temporarily blocks DOJ release of Biden tapes with biographer
item.description: An appeals court blocked the Justice Department from disclosing transcripts and recordings of former President Joe Biden's discussions with his biographer for 10 days.

item.title: Madison Square Garden database tracked celebrities' sexuality, race and "risk" level, WIRED reports
item.description: A major database breach at James Dolan's Madison Square Garden arena revealed an apparent internal list tracking nearly 40,000 celebrities, according to a new report from WIRED. The report alleges that surveillance labels included "LGBTQIA," "DO NOT HOST," and evaluated individuals on a "risk" level. MSG claims the report is inaccurate. WIRED contributing editor Noah Shachtman joins "CBS News 24/7" to discuss his reporting.

item.title: At least 12 killed by wildfire in southern Spain
item.description: The cause of the blaze hasn't been determined, but Spain has been among the many European nations hit by severe heat waves, with temperatures peaking at almost 106 in the country's south.

item.title: Hegseth frustrated with lack of adherence to grooming rules
item.description: Eight months ago, Hegseth told top military leaders there would be "no more beardos" and "fat troops."
